title: [3] MCP Server Phase 2 — Go Scaffold + Read-Only Tools
why_we_need_this: Phase 1 (task #2, completed) produced `mcp/docs/tool-surface.md` — the confirmed MCP-tool-to-`/api/*`-endpoint mapping, split into read-only and mutating tools. Per the owner-approved 5-phase roadmap (`project-context/routes/mcp-server/README.md`), Phase 2 is the first code-writing phase: scaffold the MCP server and ship ONLY the read-only tools, so the MCP-to-dashboard round trip (stdio transport, tunnel HTTP client, tool schema) is validated with zero mutation risk before any destructive tool exists. Language is Go (owner decision, 2026-07-06) — matches the dashboard's stack, single static binary, no new runtime dependency in a solo-maintained repo.
acceptance_criteria:
- Read `project-context/routes/mcp-server/README.md` in full (route rules: wrapper-only architecture, stdio transport, hardcoded-with-override target, laptop-side, no Docker) plus `mcp/docs/tool-surface.md` (Phase 1 deliverable) to get the confirmed read-only tool list and their endpoint mappings. Use `search_project_context` to cross-check against `metrics-monitoring/README.md` and `service-host-health/README.md` for the exact endpoint surface — do not derive it solely from re-reading `server.go`.
- Research and select a Go MCP SDK/library that implements the MCP stdio transport (there is no prior decision in this repo on which one — investigate current options, e.g. official `modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk` if it exists, or the best-maintained community alternative). Document which one was chosen and why in a short note (e.g. `mcp/README.md` or doc-comment), and pin its version EXACTLY in `go.mod` (no `^`, no ranges) per this repo's version-pinning convention.
- Scaffold a new Go module under `mcp/` (e.g. `mcp/go.mod`, `mcp/cmd/mcp-server/main.go`) that starts an MCP server over stdio transport, spawned on-demand as a subprocess (no long-running listener).
- Implement ONLY the read-only tools from Phase 1's tool-surface doc: metrics (system/traffic/per-client), service status, server/identity info, alerts, snapshot, geo. Do NOT implement any `/api/clients*` mutating tool (add/edit/delete/enable/disable) — those are explicitly Phase 3 and out of scope here.
- Each read-only tool's implementation must call the dashboard's existing `/api/*` endpoint via HTTP — never touch SQLite, `wg`, or any on-box file directly (wrapper-only invariant from the mcp-server route).
- HTTP client target address: default to `172.16.15.1:8080` (the confirmed WireGuard tunnel IP, per `service-host-health/README.md`), overridable via an env var or CLI flag (e.g. `MCP_DASHBOARD_ADDR`) — mirror the dashboard's own `defaultListenAddr`-with-override precedent (`dashboard/cmd/wireguard-dashboard/main.go`) rather than a bare unconfigurable literal.
- No Docker, no always-on HTTP/SSE listener — stdio subprocess transport only.
- Include a short manual-invocation note (e.g. how to point an MCP host's `mcpServers` config at this binary) but do NOT claim it has been validated against the real production dashboard/tunnel — live validation against the actual tunnel is explicitly Phase 4, not this task.
- All new files live under `mcp/` only.
important_constraints:
- Phase 2 scope is strictly read-only tools. Any mutating/`/api/clients*` write-tool code is out of scope — do not implement, even partially or behind a flag.
- Do not resolve the confirmation-gate question for mutating tools (explicit param vs. dry-run tool) — that stays deferred to Phase 3, carried over from Phase 1.
- No Docker, no always-on HTTP/SSE listener — stdio subprocess transport only (`project-context/routes/mcp-server/README.md` L41).
- Do not modify `dashboard/` or `terraform/` — this task only adds files under `mcp/`.
- Do not add any application-layer auth — the no-auth model is a settled, owner-accepted risk (tunnel + admin-only knowledge); do not re-raise or attempt to add a gate.
- Do not edit anything under `project-context/` directly — that's Praxis-managed via `update_project_context`, out of scope for this task.
- Follow this repo's git conventions once code is committed: PR-based workflow, `feat:` commit prefix, `feat/`-prefixed branch name.
- Pin all new Go module dependency versions exactly in `go.mod` — no `~>`/range-style versions.
- After this task's work lands, run `update_project_context` (per the standard MCP lifecycle) so the mcp-server route reflects that Phase 2 code now exists — do not leave the route saying "no code exists" once this ships.
